name: Primary Surface Layout
description: A static AgentSite layout proof that keeps the primary functional module first and collapses secondary modules.
brief: Create a static GitHub Pages site that demonstrates a primary-surface layout for dense AgentSite compositions. The chief-of-staff briefing should appear first after the hero, while supporting modules such as requests, runs, atlas, search, roadmap, and artifacts remain available as collapsible secondary sections. It must not add live monitoring, private tickets, raw chats, auth, analytics, persistence claims, telemetry, or a database.
heroHeadline: A primary-first layout for dense AgentSite control pages
heroLede: The page keeps the chief-of-staff briefing as the main decision surface and folds supporting modules into accessible secondary panels.
audience: Technical reviewers evaluating dense static control pages
visualDirection: dark cockpit primary briefing with collapsible supporting modules

sections.title: Primary modules should not be buried
sections.body: The selected surface appears before secondary modules so the page starts with the user's actual task.

sections.title: Supporting surfaces stay reachable
sections.body: Secondary modules remain accessible through semantic collapsible sections instead of expanding the whole page by default.

sections.title: Collapsing is layout, not data behavior
sections.body: The layout does not add persistence, auth, analytics, live sync, telemetry, private data, or database behavior.

proofArtifacts.label: Layout check
proofArtifacts.body: scripts/check-layout.mjs validates primary module placement and secondary module wrappers.

proofArtifacts.label: Primary briefing
proofArtifacts.body: src/components/ChiefOfStaffBriefing.astro renders first while secondary modules remain available.

allowedClaims: Primary functional module appears before supporting modules
Repo-local QA scripts validate primary-surface layout
approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Hiding approval boundaries, no-payment mode, or public-safety caveats
Adding live monitoring, server runtime, authentication, telemetry, persistence, or a database
